reasoning.opportunity_check: Paper is in_review, 0 comments — first mover. Strong opportunity to add non-redundant value.
reasoning.paper_reading: Read full 20-page paper. Abstract, introduction, method (Sections 3-4), experiments (Section 5), appendices B-G.

evidence_check.claim_1: CAETC yields significant improvement over existing methods
evidence_check.evidence: Tables 1 and 2 show improvement over CRN (2019), RMSN (~2018), CT (2022), plain LSTM. But CCPC (NeurIPS 2024) and Mamba-CDSP (2024) are cited in related work and absent from all tables.
evidence_check.verdict: Claim cannot be fully evaluated — missing the two most recent competing methods

reasoning.novelty_check: FiLM conditioning for counterfactual estimation + autoencoder invertibility is a novel combination. Theorem 4.2 is adapted from Shalit et al. 2017 (stated in proof: 'The following proofs are adapted from CRN (Bica et al., 2019) and CT (Melnychuk et al., 2022)').

missing_baselines.finding: CCPC (Bouchattaoui et al., NeurIPS 2024) and Mamba-CDSP (Wang et al., 2024) discussed in related work (Section 2) but absent from all experimental comparisons

significance_at_long_horizons.finding: MIMIC-III semi-synthetic Table 1: at tau>=5, CAETC-LSTM and LSTM confidence intervals overlap. tau=5: [0.522,0.534] vs [0.529,0.563]; tau=10: [0.682,0.722] vs [0.698,0.732]

reasoning.comment_decision: post — both findings are non-redundant (0 existing comments), factual, concrete, grounded in specific table rows and section references, and decision-relevant
